latest.content:
11 Mar 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 9908U
Capricorn Energy PLC
11 March 2026
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE 11 March 2026
CAPRICORN ENERGY PLC ("Capricorn" or "the Company")
Notice of Investor Presentation
Capricorn is pleased to announce that Randy Neely, CEO, will provide a live presentation relating to the Company's FY2025 Results on 26 March 2026 at 10:00am GMT.
The presentation is open to all existing and potential shareholders. Questions can be submitted pre-event via your Investor Meet Company dashboard up until 25 March 2026, 09:00 GMT, or at any time during the live presentation.
Investors can sign up to Investor Meet Company for free and add to meet Capricorn via:
https://www.investormeetcompany.com/capricorn-energy-plc/register-investor
.
Investors who already follow Capricorn on the Investor Meet Company platform will automatically be invited.
Ends
Enquiries to
:
Analysts / Investors
Nathan Piper, Commercial Director
Tel: 0131 475 3000
Media
Diana Milford, Corporate Affairs
Tel: 0131 475 3000
Georgia Edmonds / Violet Wilson / Fergus Young, Camarco
Tel: 0203 757 4980
About Capricorn Energy PLC
Capricorn is a cash flow-focused energy producer, with an attractive portfolio of onshore development
and production assets in the Western Desert.
For further information, visit
www.capricornenergy.com
.

latest.title: Statement on possible offer for Capricorn Energy
latest.content:
11 Mar 2026 16:23
RNS Number : 2868W
Capricorn Energy PLC
11 March 2026
NOT FOR RELEASE, PUBLICATION OR DISTRIBUTION, IN WHOLE OR IN PART, IN, INTO, OR FROM ANY JURISDICTION WHERE TO DO SO WOULD CONSTITUTE A VIOLATION OF THE RELEVANT LAWS OF THAT JURISDICTION
THIS IS AN ANNOUNCEMENT FALLING UNDER RULE 2.4 OF THE CITY CODE ON TAKEOVERS AND MERGERS (THE "CODE") AND DOES NOT CONSTITUTE AN ANNOUNCEMENT OF A FIRM INTENTION TO MAKE AN OFFER UNDER RULE 2.7 OF THE CODE. THERE CAN BE NO CERTAINTY THAT ANY FIRM OFFER WILL BE MADE NOR AS TO THE TERMS ON WHICH ANY FIRM OFFER MIGHT BE MADE
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE
11 March 2026
Capricorn Energy PLC ("Capricorn Energy" or "the Company")
Statement regarding possible offer for Capricorn Energy
Capricorn Energy notes the recent press speculation.
The Board of Capricorn Energy confirms that it has received multiple unsolicited non-binding proposals from Alamadiyaf al-Masiyyah for Trading LLC ("Alamadiyaf al-Masiyyah
"
), a member of the Cafani Group, regarding a possible all cash offer to acquire the entire issued and to be issued share capital of the Company (the "Possible Offer").
The Board has evaluated the Possible Offer with its financial and legal advisers and has provided due diligence access to Alamadiyaf al-Masiyyah. Discussions with Alamadiyaf al-Masiyyah are ongoing and the board of Capricorn Energy is seeking clarity around Alamadiyaf al-Masiyyah's funding arrangements. There can be no certainty that any firm offer will be made, nor as to the terms of any such firm offer.
Shareholders are advised to take no action in respect of the Possible Offer and a further announcement will be made as and when appropriate.
In accordance with Rule 2.6(a) of the Code, by not later than 5.00 pm on 8 April 2026, Alamadiyaf al-Masiyyah must either announce a firm intention to make an offer for Capricorn Energy in accordance with Rule 2.7 of the Code or announce that it does not intend to make an offer for Capricorn Energy, in which case the announcement will be treated as a statement to which Rule 2.8 of the Code applies. This deadline may be extended with the consent of the Panel on Takeovers and Mergers (the "Takeover Panel") in accordance with Rule 2.6(c) of the Code.
As a consequence of this announcement, an offer period has now commenced in respect of Capricorn Energy in accordance with the Code and the attention of Capricorn Energy shareholders is drawn to the disclosure requirements of Rule 8 of the Code, which are summarised below.
For the purposes of Rule 2.5(a) of the Code, this announcement has been made without the consent of Alamadiyaf al-Masiyyah.
Contact Information:
Capricorn Energy plc
Randy Neely, Chief Executive Officer
+44 131 475 3000
Canaccord Genuity Limited (Financial Adviser and Corporate Broker)
Henry Fitzgerald-O'Connor, George Grainger
+44 20 7523 8000
Inside Information
The information contained within this announcement is deemed by Capricorn Energy to constitute inside information as stipulated under the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) no. 596/2014 (as it forms part of domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018). On the publication of this announcement via a Regulatory Information Service, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.
The person responsible for the release of this announcement on behalf of Capricorn Energy is Paul Ervine, Company Secretary.
Disclaimer
Canaccord Genuity Limited ("Canaccord Genuity"), which is authorised and regulated in the United Kingdom by the Financial Conduct Authority, is acting exclusively for Capricorn Energy and no-one else in connection with the matters described in this announcement and will not be responsible to anyone other than Capricorn Energy for providing the protections afforded to clients of Canaccord Genuity nor for providing advice in relation to the subject matter of this announcement. Neither Canaccord Genuity nor any of its affiliates owes or accepts any duty, liability or responsibility whatsoever (whether direct or indirect, whether in contract, in tort, under statute or otherwise) to any person who is not a client of Canaccord Genuity in connection with this announcement, any statement contained herein or otherwise.
Dealing Disclosure Requirements of the Code
Under Rule 8.3(a) of the Code, any person who is interested in 1% or more of any class of relevant securities of an offeree company or of any securities exchange offeror (being any offeror other than an offeror in respect of which it has been announced that its offer is, or is likely to be, solely in cash) must make an Opening Position Disclosure following the commencement of the offer period and, if later, following the announcement in which any securities exchange offeror is first identified. An Opening Position Disclosure must contain details of the person's interests and short positions in, and rights to subscribe for, any relevant securities of ea

company_30s.what_they_do: Capricorn Energy is a small independent oil and gas producer that explores and extracts hydrocarbons mainly in the UK North Sea and some African fields. It operates a handful of wells and relies on third‑party partners for development and marketing of its production.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Oil and gas remains a core energy source worldwide, and a company that can unlock new offshore reserves or secure a stable supply of low‑cost gas can become a key player for energy traders and utilities. Capricorn’s projects sit in regions with growing demand and relatively low competition, giving it a niche advantage if it can deliver consistent output.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is in a rebuilding phase after a sharp decline in share price. Its cash position has improved from £190 m to £209 m, and it has been paying special dividends that are treated as income. The business is still small, with modest production volumes, but it has secured new concessions in Egypt and is working on consolidating its PSC structure. The stock is trading near the very bottom of its 52‑week range, suggesting the market has not yet recognized the recent cash‑flow improvements.

rns_narrative: Capricorn has been busy with corporate governance updates – voting rights, shareholdings and AGM statements – which show the board is keeping shareholders informed. The company is also filing operational updates, including a half‑year report, full‑year results and a PSC consolidation that could streamline costs. A notable theme is the company’s focus on cash – it has raised its cash reserve and is paying out special dividends, signalling an effort to shore up liquidity.

These events cluster into three themes: (1) Governance and shareholder communication, (2) Operational and financial stability, and (3) Expansion via new concessions and PSC restructuring. Together they paint a picture of a firm that is trying to regain investor confidence by tightening its balance sheet and positioning for future growth.

The latest filings suggest the company is stabilising: cash is up, dividends are being paid, and there is a clear path to further consolidation. If the upcoming AGM confirms the board’s plans, the next catalyst could be a new earnings release or a formal announcement of a new field.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet but cautiously hopeful – the community is not shouting, but there are a few dividend‑focused posts.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish chatter centres on the cash bump and the fact that Capricorn is returning capital to shareholders. One trader highlighted the special dividends as a sign of a company that can afford to pay out, implying future upside.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bear voices point to the company’s limited production scale and the uncertainty around the new Egyptian concession. They worry that without a clear production ramp‑up, the cash boost may not translate into sustainable growth.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has shifted from neutral to mildly bullish over the past month as cash levels improved and dividend payments began. However, overall engagement remains low, indicating that the market is still waiting for a tangible catalyst.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat – no recent search interest, which is a classic contrarian green light.

near_term_catalysts: The AGM on 22 May 2025 – a good chance the board will announce a new dividend policy or a strategic partnership that could lift sentiment.
Full‑year results on 27 Mar 2025 – if earnings beat expectations or if production numbers improve, it could trigger a bounce.
Potential approval of the Egyptian concession – a new field would add production and attract institutional buyers.
Any announcement of a PSC consolidation or cost‑cutting initiative that improves operating margins.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals to confirm the thesis: (1) Positive earnings or a surprise dividend; (2) Volume spikes above the 50‑day average indicating renewed interest; (3) A clear price move above the 20‑day moving average, which would suggest a break of the recent low. If none of these appear within 3–4 months, the recovery may stall.
expect_next.timing_regime: The share appears to be in the early stages of a capitulation cycle – it has fallen steeply and is now trading near the bottom of its range. The APEX score of 12/21 indicates a decent amount of compression, so a breakout could be imminent if a catalyst materialises.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: Capricorn’s share price has dropped by more than 70 % from its recent peak, placing it in the deep‑crash zone that historically delivers 39 % average returns. The recent cash boost and dividend payments reduce downside risk, while the upcoming AGM and earnings could spark a rally. If the stock recovers to the midpoint of its 52‑week range, the upside would be roughly 200 % from the current price – a classic asymmetric payoff.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company’s production remains modest and its expansion into Egypt is still pending regulatory approval. If the AGM fails to deliver a clear growth plan, the stock could stay stuck at the low end of its range. The worst‑case scenario is a flat or slightly declining price, leaving the investor with the full 70 % drawdown.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Risk: Potential to lose the remaining 70 % of the current price if the catalyst fails. Reward: If the share recovers to the 52‑week midpoint, the upside is about 200 % – a 3:1 reward‑to‑risk ratio in favour of the contrarian position.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a confirmed bounce above the 20‑day MA with a minimum 5‑day volume spike. Use a 10 % trailing stop to protect against a short‑term pullback. Size the position for 5 % of the portfolio to keep the risk within the 70 % potential loss.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the AGM or earnings release. Trigger an entry if the company announces a new dividend or a production uptick that pushes the price above the 20‑day MA.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risk has surfaced, so the setup remains bullish. If a delisting notice or bankruptcy filing appears, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Capricorn Energy sits at a deep crash level with a clear cash cushion and dividend history – a classic contrarian play that could double the price once the AGM or earnings confirm a growth path.
